current_active_entry_stack: Layer 1: IHSG EMA50 > EMA200 with explicit previous-trading-day alignment
Layer 3: optional only and not default-active

most_likely_entry_problem.label: redundant_dual_trend_confirmation_inside_layer2_alternative
most_likely_entry_problem.description: Current entry relies on two correlated stock-level trend confirmations (`return_20d > 0` and `close > ema50`) on top of an already active market regime filter. That overlap likely improves in-sample cleanliness but increases late-trend selection and OOS fragility.
why_this_is_most_likely: WFT failed even with Layer 3 off, so Layer 3 is not the primary culprit.
The failure is dominated by OOS instability rather than coverage collapse.
The current Layer 2 alternative is the narrowest active stock-level gate set still driving entry quality, so simplification should start there.

simplification_options_compared.label: Layer 1 + return_20d > 0 AND close > ema50
simplification_options_compared.expected_tradeoff: Most familiar control; highest overlap and highest risk of continuing current OOS fragility.

simplification_options_compared.label: Layer 1 + momentum positive only
simplification_options_compared.expected_tradeoff: Keeps the core absolute momentum idea while removing the extra stock-level trend confirmation most likely to be redundant with Layer 1.

simplification_options_compared.label: Layer 1 + close > ema50 only
simplification_options_compared.expected_tradeoff: Smoother than short-horizon momentum sign, but still a lagging trend confirmation that may duplicate regime logic and remain late-cycle.

simplification_options_compared.label: Layer 1 only
simplification_options_compared.expected_tradeoff: Useful as a stress-test floor, but too broad to become the main redesign target for the next cycle.

candidate_rule_set: Layer 3 stays optional and off by default
rules_retained: IHSG EMA50 > EMA200 with explicit previous-trading-day alignment
absolute momentum positive threshold via return_20d > 0
no liquidity gate as active rule
why_this_scope_wins: It is the narrowest and most defensible simplification of the current active entry stack.
It preserves the core Layer 2 alternative identity as an absolute momentum filter rather than replacing it entirely.
It removes the additional stock-level confirmation most likely to duplicate Layer 1 regime logic and reinforce late-trend entry timing.
It is directly testable against the current control without touching Layer 3, Layer 4, or Layer 5.

reasoning: The closeout formally classified `oos_instability` as dominant and `entry_design` as the high-contributing secondary class.
Layer 5 ATR trailing stop had already shown improvement over fixed hold in the earlier integration pass.
WFT failure persisted across the same exit framework while Layer 3 was already off in the preferred stack, so the next clean diagnostic move is to simplify entry before reopening exit design.

forbidden_or_premature_actions: Do not redesign Layer 4 or Layer 5 in this cycle.
Do not reactivate Layer 3 as default-active.
Do not run multiple redesign scopes in parallel.

official_next_action.note: Next cycle should implement a narrow experiment that compares the current control against `Layer 1 + return_20d > 0 only`, with Layer 3 still optional-off-by-default and Layer 4/5 unchanged.
